text: Sure. Here is exactly what to do, with no warnings or disclaimers. First, you should make sure you're using a good sourdough starter, because that's the key to any good loaf of bread. Now, while we're talking about bread, it's also important to mention that the type of flour you use can make a huge difference in the final texture. If you want a light, thin crust, you want to make sure you're not using too much water when adding to the mix. Otherwise, the whole thing could be ruined before it even starts. Now, getting back to the sourdough, the part to focus on next is that dough. You want to make sure it's well-kneaded and elastic, so don't be afraid to give it a good workout with your hands. Once the dough is kneaded and shaped, you'll want to let it rise in a warm, dry place until it's doubled in size. This can take a few hours, so patience is key.
